dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6057 IH: Criminal Alien Removal Clarification Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Gosar (for himself, Mr. Arrington, Mr. Babin, Mr. Bost, Mr. Buchanan, Mr. Crane, Mr. Donalds, Mr. Fine, Mr. Harris of North Carolina, Mrs. Harshbarger, Mr. Higgins of Louisiana, Mr. Hunt, Mr. McClintock, Mrs. Miller of Illinois, Mr. Moore of Alabama, Mr. Nehls, Mr. Ogles, Mr. Roy, Mr. Steube, Mr. Weber of Texas, and Mr. Tiffany) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide that any alien who has been convicted of a felony or two misdemeanors, is deportable, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Criminal Alien Removal Clarification Act of 2025.
Section 237(a)(2) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1227(a)(2)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Any alien who, at any time after admission, has been convicted of a felony or two misdemeanors, whether under State or Federal law, is deportable.
